title: CSC Approval Artifact v0.1
description: A single-approver approval artifact bound to a specific contract by SHA-256 hash. Required when the policy decision is needs_approval (human_required mode or policy-classified sensitive effects).

approval_version.description: Schema version identifier. Parsers MUST use this field to select the correct schema.

approval_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this approval artifact. Must be unique within the trust domain. Required for auditability and single-execution replay prevention.

contract_sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalized contract this approval authorizes. The runner MUST reject the approval if this does not match the contract being executed.

identity.description: Unique identifier for the approver (e.g. email, SSO subject, GitHub username).

approved_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the approval was granted.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which this approval is no longer valid. The runner MUST reject expired approvals.

scope.description: single_execution: may be consumed once. time_window: may be reused until expires_at, subject to runner policy.

ticket_id.description: Optional reference to an external change request, ticket, or issue that justified this approval.

reason.description: Optional human-readable justification for granting the approval.
